unit.name: Automatic evaluation metrics for NLG
unit.description: Introduces lexical-overlap and embedding-based automatic metrics, along with their main strengths and failure modes.
unit.summary: The segment begins the evaluation portion by separating methods into content-overlap metrics, model-based metrics, and human evaluations [ts=3310s]. It explains that lexical-overlap metrics such as BLEU and ROUGE are cheap, fast, and widely used, especially in machine translation and summarization [ts=3323s]. Their weakness is that they operate on word overlap: they can miss semantically equivalent paraphrases and can also reward outputs with high lexical overlap but wrong meaning, yielding both false negatives and false positives [ts=3349s] [ts=3460s]. The lecture emphasizes that these metrics degrade as tasks become more open-ended, from summarization to dialogue to story generation [ts=3387s]. It then transitions to model-based metrics that compare texts through learned embeddings rather than raw n-grams [ts=3515s]. Examples include vector similarity, word mover's distance using optimal transport, BERTScore using contextual embeddings and alignment, sentence-embedding similarity, and BLEURT as a BERT-based regression model for text quality relative to a reference [ts=3582s] [ts=3627s] [ts=3678s].

key_points.text: Automatic evaluation splits into content-overlap metrics, model-based metrics, and human evaluations.
BLEU and ROUGE are cheap and fast but rely on lexical overlap with a reference.
Lexical-overlap metrics suffer both false negatives for paraphrases and false positives for wrong answers with overlapping words.
Content-overlap metrics become progressively worse as NLG tasks become more open-ended.
Model-based metrics use learned embeddings to measure semantic similarity rather than raw n-gram overlap.
Word mover's distance aligns source and target embeddings with optimal transport.
BERTScore and BLEURT are embedding-based metrics that use contextual alignment or BERT regression to score generated text.

kp_catalog.name: Automatic evaluation metrics for NLG
kp_catalog.description: The landscape of reference-based lexical and embedding-based automatic metrics used to score generated text.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because automatic evaluation metrics for NLG clarifies the landscape of reference-based lexical and embedding-based automatic metrics used to score generated text, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
